edges.description: Senescent chondrocytes reinforce inflammatory and matrix-degrading programs that accelerate cartilage loss.
Synovitis-associated macrophage-fibroblast signaling amplifies inflammatory mediators that promote cartilage matrix breakdown.

nodes.description: Osteoarthritis cartilage shows a maladaptive chondrocyte state with increased matrix-degrading enzymes, especially collagenases and aggrecanases, leading to progressive breakdown of the articular cartilage extracellular matrix.

evidence.reference_title: Elevated expression of periostin in human osteoarthritic cartilage and its potential role in matrix degradation via matrix metalloproteinase-13.
evidence.snippet: In OA, dysregulated gene expression and phenotypic changes in articular chondrocytes culminate in progressive loss of cartilage from the joint surface.

evidence.reference_title: Elevated expression of periostin in human osteoarthritic cartilage and its potential role in matrix degradation via matrix metalloproteinase-13.
evidence.snippet: These findings implicate periostin as a catabolic protein that promotes cartilage degeneration in OA by up-regulating MMP-13 through canonical Wnt signaling.

nodes.description: Aging-associated chondrocyte senescence promotes inflammatory and catabolic signaling within articular cartilage and accelerates osteoarthritis progression.

evidence.reference_title: Sirt6 attenuates chondrocyte senescence and osteoarthritis progression.
evidence.snippet: Here we show that Sirt6 deficiency exaggerates chondrocyte senescence and osteoarthritis progression, whereas intra-articular injection of adenovirus-Sirt6 markedly attenuates surgical destabilization of medial meniscus-induced osteoarthritis.

nodes.description: Low-grade synovitis in osteoarthritis is sustained by interactions between recruited macrophages and fibroblast-like synovial cells, amplifying inflammatory signaling, stromal activation, pain, and tissue remodeling.

evidence.reference_title: Monocyte-Derived Macrophages-Synovial Fibroblasts Crosstalk Unravels Oncostatin Signaling Network as a Driver of Synovitis in Osteoarthritis.
evidence.snippet: In individuals with OA, single-cell transcriptomics identified a conserved MERTKlow CD48high CCR2+ macrophage subpopulation as a key source of OSM.

evidence.reference_title: Monocyte-Derived Macrophages-Synovial Fibroblasts Crosstalk Unravels Oncostatin Signaling Network as a Driver of Synovitis in Osteoarthritis.
evidence.snippet: Specifically, the OSM/OSMR axis represents a pivotal mechanism in recruited macrophage-fibroblast crosstalk, offering potential targets for novel biomarkers and therapies to manage OA-related synovitis.
